reasoning:
The forecast asks whether the closing price of LW on Jan 25 and Feb 17 will exceed its last recorded close on the forecast date (42.97). Short-term stock returns follow a near-random walk, typically giving a base rate probability of around ~53% for an increase over 1-4 week periods. 
1. For Jan 25: The momentum has been slightly positive since the stock's massive drop in December (recovering from ~41 to ~43). With no specific known catalysts between Jan 18 and Jan 25, I allocate a standard base-rate probability of 0.53. The statistical tool estimates 0.57 relying heavily on a single seasonal data point of N=1, which I discount to stay closer to the natural base rate.
2. For Feb 17: The company recently authorized a dividend increase to $0.38 per share. The record date is scheduled for Friday, January 30, 2026, which makes the ex-dividend date January 29. On this date, the stock is expected to face a mechanical drag of approximately $0.38 (almost 0.9% of the share price). Over a 1-month horizon, a 0.9% headwind is enough to overcome the normal upward market drift, slightly reducing the probability of an absolute price increase. Thus, I assign 0.48 for this date.
